The Mechanism of Sex Determination is Different Between Species
Birds, some fishes and moths
ZW system
Female is heterogametic

The Mechanism of Sex Determination is Different Between Species
In Drosophila the X to autosome ratio determines sex
A ratio is 1.0 or greater, the organism is a female.  When the ration is 0.5 or less its male  If  the ratio is 0.67, then it is an intersex animal.
1X:2A  0.5 =M
2X:2A  1.0 =F
1X:3A 0.33=M
3X:2A  1.5 =F
2X:3A  0.66-M/F intersex

The Mechanism of Sex Determination is Different Between Species
Some single cell organisms have mating types
Yeast:  a and a
Chlamydomonas:   +  and  -

Sex Linked
Genes found on a specific sex chromosome

Sex Influenced
The traits that appear in both sexes but either their frequency of occurrence in the two sexes is different or there is a different relationship between genotype and phenotype

Sex-limited
A genetically controlled character that is phenotypically exhibited in only one of the two sexes
